Economist · Computational Policy Researcher · Civic Data Scientist

Marcy
Castro Lívano

Data can reveal the invisible structures of social problems.
I build the tools to find them.

I am a Peruvian economist and MSc student at the University of Chicago Harris School, working at the intersection of data science, public policy, and civic technology.

Marcy Castro Lívano© 2026 · Chicago, IL
Background
About Me

I grew up in Peru asking questions about how societies work — why some public systems function and others don't, why well-designed policies so often fail in practice, and what it actually takes to change institutional outcomes. Those questions led me to economics, and economics led me to nearly a decade of applied research across Latin America, West Africa, and the United States.

I have designed field evaluations for national-scale RCTs in Sierra Leone, built data pipelines for public institutions in Lima, automated analytical workflows for the IDB, and contributed to impact evaluations at J-PAL and the World Bank.

That's what brought me to the MSc in Computational Analysis and Public Policy at UChicago — not a career pivot, but a deepening.

Selected Work
Projects
01Active · 2025–26

CivicPulse Chicago

When residents file 311 complaints about broken lights, potholes, or failing infrastructure — does the city listen equally? And does the budget reflect what people actually need?

CivicPulse integrates Chicago's 311 service requests, census demographics, and public budget allocations to map where institutional responsiveness breaks down.

PythonGeoPandasPlotlyCensus APISpatial AnalysisDashboard
GitHubDashboard
Key Themes
→ Spatial inequality in service delivery
→ Budget–complaint misalignment by neighbourhood
→ Demographic patterns in civic voice
→ Composite civic responsiveness score
02
GitHub

ETL Pipeline — National Pension Office

How do you turn 45,000 noisy legal-case records into an operational intelligence system for 200+ attorneys?

End-to-end automated ETL pipeline transforming fragmented case data into a validated unified database. Real-time Power BI dashboards reduced reporting time by 40%.

PythonPandasPower BIPower QueryETL
03

Field Data Systems — Sierra Leone RCT

How do you build rigorous data infrastructure across 450 communities in a fragile system?

Programmed complex ODK survey instruments for 7,000+ households and built real-time supervision dashboards for 150 enumerators. Contributed to evidence of a 26pp increase in vaccination uptake.

ODK / SurveyCTOStataPower calculationsRCT design
04
GitHub

Probabilistic Record Linkage — IDB

Millions of citizen records, no shared ID. How do you link them without losing the people who fall through the cracks?

Built a Python fuzzy-matching and probabilistic record-linkage algorithm for the IDB, reducing false matches and supporting 20–30 analysts on two national RCTs.

PythonFuzzy matchingStataCausal inference

Get in Touch
Contact

I am currently based in Chicago, finishing my MSc and working as a Research Assistant at UChicago's Development Innovation Lab. I am actively looking for summer 2026 internships and post-graduation opportunities in civic tech, public interest data science, policy research, and applied development economics.

If you are working on problems at the intersection of data, institutions, and accountability — in government, research, or technology — I would like to hear from you.
